audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 1 Samuel 7 draft aligned to Kiriath-jearim custody, Israel's return, Mizpah, battle, Ebenezer, territorial recovery, peace, and Samuel's circuit; later source/witness audit required.
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Hebrew-first naming, restored/mystical separation, narrative ambiguity, institutional power, vulnerable persons, violence, and misuse risks.

verses.restored: Humans of Kiriath-jearim came and brought up ark of YHWH. They brought it to house of Abinadab on hill and consecrated Eleazar his son to keep ark of YHWH.
It happened from day ark rested at Kiriath-jearim that days multiplied and became twenty years. All house of Israel lamented/longed after YHWH.

Samuel said to all house of Israel, If with all your heart you are returning to YHWH, remove foreign gods and Ashtaroth from among you, establish your heart toward YHWH, and serve him alone; then he will rescue you from hand of Philistines.
Children of Israel removed Baals and Ashtaroth and served YHWH alone.
Samuel said, Gather all Israel at Mizpah, and I will pray for you to YHWH.
They gathered at Mizpah, drew water and poured it before YHWH, fasted on that day, and said there, We have sinned against YHWH. Samuel judged children of Israel at Mizpah.
Philistines heard that children of Israel had gathered at Mizpah, and lords of Philistines went up against Israel. Children of Israel heard and were afraid before Philistines.
Children of Israel said to Samuel, Do not be silent from crying out for us to YHWH our God, that he may deliver us from hand of Philistines.
Samuel took one sucking lamb and offered it whole as an ascending offering to YHWH. Samuel cried out to YHWH for Israel, and YHWH answered him.

As Samuel was offering ascending offering, Philistines drew near for battle against Israel. YHWH thundered with a great voice on that day against Philistines, confused them, and they were struck before Israel.
Humans of Israel went out from Mizpah, pursued Philistines, and struck them as far as below Beth-car.
Samuel took one stone and set it between Mizpah and Shen. He called its name Ebenezer, saying, Thus far YHWH has helped us.
Philistines were subdued and did not continue to enter border of Israel. Hand of YHWH was against Philistines all days of Samuel.
Cities that Philistines had taken from Israel returned to Israel, from Ekron as far as Gath, and Israel rescued their territory from hand of Philistines. There was peace between Israel and Amorites.
Samuel judged Israel all days of his life.
He went from year to year and made circuit to Bethel, Gilgal, and Mizpah, and judged Israel in all these places.
His return was to Ramah, for there was his house, and there he judged Israel. He built there an altar to YHWH.

translation_notes: Kiriath-jearim receives and houses the ark for twenty years. Sacred custody is responsibility, not ownership of Divine presence.
Israel's lamenting or longing after YHWH conveys communal desire and grief, not ethnic purity.

Samuel's command to remove Baals and Ashtaroth belongs to Israel's covenant exclusivity. It may not authorize religious persecution, destruction of others' sacred property, coercive conversion, or antisemitic/supersessionist claims.
Public confession is communal practice, not permission to compel individual disclosure or stage humiliation.
Fasting and pouring water are ritual actions whose exact meanings require audit; they are not mandatory techniques for earning deliverance.
Philistine military action is reported without making all Philistines timeless enemies.
Samuel's intercession does not establish clergy as gatekeepers who can sell access to God.
The sucking lamb sacrifice reflects an ancient animal-offering system and should not be reenacted or sentimentalized.
Thunder and battle confusion are narrated as Divine aid, but the ensuing pursuit remains violence and cannot become a universal holy-war model.
Ebenezer means stone of help. Memorials should remember dependence and loss, not erase casualties or become territorial triumphalism.
Territorial recovery and peace with Amorites are both reported; peace is not lesser than military success.
Samuel's circuit shows distributed access to judgment, though the narrative does not audit whose voices were excluded.
Samuel builds an altar at Ramah; this should not override later centralization traditions or authorize private religious domination.
safety_note: Do not use covenant exclusivity to persecute other religions or destroy sacred objects. Do not glorify war, animal sacrifice, coerced confession, or territorial domination. Ebenezer remembers help, not ethnic superiority. Philistines and Amorites remain human communities, and peace is a central good.
